summary: Frontend-only mobile testing on Expo web preview. Splashâ†’Dashboardâ†’Connectâ†’Measureâ†’Calibrateâ†’Historyâ†’Settings core flows WORK. Fixes from iter1 confirmed: /connect route loads (no 404), AsyncStorage causes no page errors, demo BLE flow completes. Live absorbance streams and updates (0.000â†’0.442 after 3s), chart renders, calibration saves with Active badge, theme toggle works, LED fixed toggle reveals fixed-wavelength-input, ALL settings persist across reload. However two testID mismatches vs the review_request block a few assertions and the native confirm dialog used for delete lacks a web-compatible testID.

ui_bugs.issue: History item testID uses prefix 'hist-{id}' (line 93) but review_request expects 'history-item-{id}'. Test selectors [data-testid^=history-item-] return 0 matches even when items are visible in UI.

Concentration readout on Measure screen has NO testID (expected 'live-concentration'). The value renders correctly (e.g. 19.19 mg/L when a calibration is active, 'â€”' otherwise) but cannot be asserted by automation. Review_request explicitly lists this ID.

Metric testIDs expected by review_request are missing: 'metric-mean-absorbance', 'metric-mean-intensity', 'metric-concentration'. Could not open detail to sanity-check but query by these testIDs returned MISSING.

Delete confirmation and Stop&Save rely on native window.confirm/alert on web. No 'confirm-delete-btn' testID exists, so automated flows must hook page.on('dialog'). Consider a custom Modal with testID 'confirm-delete-btn' for consistency.

integration_issues.issue: During automated run, typing 'TEST' into history-search while a matching item ('TEST-002') was in the list caused the visible items to hide. Could be controlled-input timing OR case-sensitive matching; recommend verifying `.toLowerCase()` on both sides of filter predicate.

issues: A wrapper element with testID 'device-card' is rendered but not visible (empty wrapper matched by [data-testid^='device-']). Causes automation to click an invisible element. Remove the generic wrapper testID or make it more specific.

react_native_issues.issue: Metro warnings: 'Too many screens defined. Route "connect" is extraneous. / Route "measurement/[id]" is extraneous'. The current _layout.tsx no longer declares them explicitly (fix applied), but warnings persist - may be cached HMR from a prior bundle; verify after full restart: sudo supervisorctl restart expo.

Expo doctor reports version mismatches: @react-native-async-storage/async-storage@3.0.2 (expected 2.2.0), expo-file-system@55.0.17 (expected ~19.0.21), expo-sharing@55.0.18 (expected ~14.0.8), react-native-svg@15.15.4 (expected 15.12.1). Iter1 context note said async-storage was downgraded to 2.2.0 but it is still 3.0.2 in node_modules.
Console warning: 'useNativeDriver is not supported because the native animated module is missing' - harmless on web but noisy.

action_items: Rename testID prefix in /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/history.tsx line 93 from `hist-${m.id}` to `history-item-${m.id}` to match review_request.
Add testID='live-concentration' to the concentration <Text> inside the LIVE MEASUREMENT card on /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/measure.tsx (the value that shows 19.19 mg/L or 'â€”').
Add testIDs on /app/frontend/app/measurement/[id].tsx for the three metric tiles: metric-mean-absorbance, metric-mean-intensity, metric-concentration.
Remove the wrapper testID 'device-card' on /app/frontend/app/connect.tsx (or move it off the invisible container) so [data-testid^='device-'] only matches real rows.

Verify async-storage version: `yarn why @react-native-async-storage/async-storage` â€” resolution shows 3.0.2 despite iter1 claim of 2.2.0 downgrade. Run `yarn add @react-native-async-storage/async-storage@2.2.0` and restart expo to align with Expo SDK 54.
Double-check history search predicate uses case-insensitive match on sample_id/contaminant/operator.
Optional: replace native window.confirm for delete with an in-app Modal carrying testID 'confirm-delete-btn' to match the review_request.
critical_code_review_comments: history.tsx line 93 testID naming inconsistent with review_request contract.
measure.tsx live concentration <Text> lacks testID, preventing assertion of concentration-with-calibration behavior.
connect.tsx exposes an invisible 'device-card' testID that conflicts with device row queries.
Package versions drifted from Expo SDK expected set; can cause subtle web-storage bugs.
seed_data_creation: Created 1 calibration profile 'Nitrate 540nm' (Active) and 1 measurement 'TEST-002' in web localStorage during test run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Demo Mode is default on web (native BLE unavailable). Splash delay ~1.4s, then tabs mount. Stop & Save and Delete use native window.confirm on web â€” always register page.on('dialog', lambda d: d.accept()). Device rows use testID 'device-demo-esp32-a' / 'device-demo-esp32-b'. History items currently use 'hist-{id}' prefix (NOT history-item). Concentration text on Measure has no testID yet. AsyncStorage works (settings persisted across page.reload), zero uncaught page errors. Mobile viewport 390x844.
rca_of_issue: testID mismatches are pure naming drift between the spec (review_request) and current implementation â€” main agent needs to align testID strings. Version drift of async-storage is likely why iter1 warnings about â€˜Native module is nullâ€™ existed; the package.json may have been updated but node_modules not refreshed.
